author: Baltasar Gracián
text: The greatest wisdom lies in knowing how to dissimulate.

author: Baltasar Gracián
text: Reputation is worth more than wealth.

author: Baltasar Gracián
text: It is not enough to be right; one must know how to use reason.

author: Baltasar Gracián
text: Not showing all your cards is a rule of prudence.

author: Baltasar Gracián
text: The wise man gains more from his enemies than the fool from his friends.

author: Baltasar Gracián
text: Think with few and speak with even fewer.

author: Baltasar Gracián
text: Knowing how to hide one's own strength is a great strength.

author: Cicero
text: To err is human, but to persist in error is foolish.

author: Popular saying
text: The ends justify the means.

author: Dostoevsky
text: Pain and suffering are always inevitable for a broad intelligence and a deep heart.

author: Dostoevsky
text: Lying is the only privilege man has over all organisms.

author: Dostoevsky
text: The secret of human existence lies not only in living, but in knowing what one lives for.

author: Edgar Allan Poe
text: The boundary that separates life from death is, at best, vague and shadowy.

author: Edgar Allan Poe
text: Believe only half of what you see and nothing that you hear.

author: Edgar Allan Poe
text: Those who dream by day know many things that escape those who dream only by night.

author: Edgar Allan Poe
text: The truly brave man does not fear appearing cowardly.

author: Epicurus
text: Necessity is an evil, but there is no necessity to live with necessity.

author: Epicurus
text: Nothing is enough for the one for whom enough is little.

author: Epictetus
text: Wealth does not consist in having much, but in wanting little.

author: Epictetus
text: Control your passions, so they do not take revenge on you.

author: Epictetus
text: It is impossible to begin to learn what one presumes to know.

author: Epictetus
text: Do not explain your philosophy; embody it.

author: Epictetus
text: No man is free unless he is master of himself.

author: Epictetus
text: First learn the meaning of what you say, then speak.

author: Epictetus
text: If you want to improve, accept appearing foolish.

author: Epictetus
text: Only the one who lives as they wish is free.

author: Aesop
text: Against the argument of force, there is no reason.

author: Francis Bacon
text: Hope is a good breakfast, but a bad supper.

author: Francis Bacon
text: Revenge is a kind of wild justice.

author: Francis Bacon
text: Knowledge is power.

author: Heraclitus
text: The true constitution of things likes to hide itself.

author: Heraclitus
text: For those who are awake, there is only one common world.

author: Julius Caesar
text: Experience is the teacher of all things.

author: Julius Caesar
text: It is easier to find men willing to die than men willing to endure pain patiently.

author: Julius Caesar
text: I would rather be first in this village than second in Rome.

author: Julius Caesar
text: I came, I saw, I conquered.

author: Laozi
text: Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom.

author: Laozi
text: Great acts are made of small deeds.

author: Laozi
text: The wise person does not show off, and therefore shines.

author: Laozi
text: Those who know how to be content are rich.

author: Laozi
text: Those who conquer others are strong; those who conquer themselves are powerful.

author: Machiavelli
text: Fortune shows its power where there is no organized virtue to resist it.

author: Machiavelli
text: It is much safer to be feared than loved, when one must choose between the two.

author: Machiavelli
text: It is necessary to learn how not to be good, and to use or not use that according to necessity.

author: Machiavelli
text: Men must either be caressed or exterminated.

author: Machiavelli
text: Whoever becomes master of a city accustomed to freedom and does not destroy it should expect to be destroyed by it.

author: Machiavelli
text: A wise prince must rely on what is his own, not on what belongs to others.

author: Marcus Aurelius
text: Nothing happens to a man that his nature is not capable of bearing.

author: Marcus Aurelius
text: Our life is what our thoughts make of it.

author: Miyamoto Musashi
text: In strategy, it is important to see what is distant as if it were near.

author: Miyamoto Musashi
text: Today I defeat the self I was yesterday.

author: Miyamoto Musashi
text: Do nothing that is useless.

author: Miyamoto Musashi
text: Do not regret what you have done.

author: Miyamoto Musashi
text: Perceive what cannot be seen with the eyes.

author: Napoleon
text: Imagination rules the world.

author: Napoleon
text: The word impossible is not in my dictionary.

author: Napoleon
text: Victory belongs to the most persevering.

author: Napoleon
text: Never interrupt your enemy when he is making a mistake.

author: Napoleon
text: A leader is a dealer in hope.

author: Plato
text: What matters most is not to live, but to live well.

author: Schopenhauer
text: Genius and madness have something in common: both live in another world.

author: Schopenhauer
text: Solitude is the fate of superior spirits.

author: Schopenhauer
text: Life swings like a pendulum, backward and forward, between pain and boredom.

author: Schopenhauer
text: Fate shuffles the cards, and we play.

author: Schopenhauer
text: The common man seeks to pass the time; the talented man seeks to use it.

author: Schopenhauer
text: The less intelligent a man is, the less mysterious existence seems to him.

author: Shakespeare
text: There is method in this madness.

author: Shakespeare
text: The false face must hide what the false heart knows.

author: Shakespeare
text: Hell is empty, and all the demons are here.

author: Shakespeare
text: Cowards die many times before their death; the valiant taste death only once.

author: Shakespeare
text: Be great in act, as you have been in thought.

author: Seneca
text: Difficulty strengthens the mind, as labor strengthens the body.

author: Seneca
text: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.

author: Seneca
text: Make haste to live well.

author: Seneca
text: Mastering oneself is the greatest empire.

author: Seneca
text: It is not because things are difficult that we do not dare; it is because we do not dare that they are difficult.

author: Seneca
text: No wind blows in favor of one who does not know where they are going.

author: Seneca
text: Whoever suffers before it is necessary suffers more than is necessary.

author: Seneca
text: If you live according to the laws of nature, you will never be poor.

author: Seneca
text: We suffer more in imagination than in reality.

author: Sun Tzu
text: He who occupies the battlefield first awaits the enemy at ease.

author: Sun Tzu
text: The supreme art of war is to subdue the enemy without fighting.

author: Sun Tzu
text: Tactics without strategy is the noise before defeat.

author: Sun Tzu
text: Victory belongs to the one who knows when to fight and when not to fight.

author: Sun Tzu
text: Know the enemy and know yourself; you will not fear the result of a hundred battles.

author: Sun Tzu
text: In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.

author: Sun Tzu
text: Appear weak when you are strong, and strong when you are weak.

author: Sun Tzu
text: All warfare is based on deception.

author: Thales of Miletus
text: Many words do not necessarily indicate much wisdom.

author: Thomas Hobbes
text: Curiosity is the lust of the mind.

author: Thomas Hobbes
text: Man is wolf to man.

author: Thomas Hobbes
text: Fear and freedom are compatible.

author: Thomas Hobbes
text: Covenants without the sword are only words.
